subject: Cloud Computing
title: Google and Microsoft Enter the Race
description: One-minute skill: Google and Microsoft Enter the Race.

hook.text: By 2008, Amazon had a two-year head start. Google and Microsoft scrambled to catch up.

buildup.text: Google launched App Engine in 2008, targeting developers who wanted zero server management.

discovery.text: Microsoft launched Azure in 2010, betting that enterprises would trust a familiar name.

twist.text: Google had the best tech but couldn't sell to businesses. Microsoft had the relationships.

climax.text: Today AWS leads, Azure is second, Google Cloud third. Three companies dominate the market.

punchline.text: Three giants split the cloud. Everyone else fights for scraps.

quiz.question: Why did Microsoft Azure gain traction despite launching years after AWS?
options: Enterprises already trusted Microsoft from decades of business software
Azure was completely free for the first five years
